title: Check Point Harmony SASE Admin Audit (Check Point)
spl:
index=checkpoint sourcetype="cp_log" product="SmartConsole" earliest=-30d
| where match(lower(operation),"(?i)create|modify|delete|publish|install")
| stats count earliest(_time) as first latest(_time) as last values(operation) as ops by administrator, object_name
| sort -last
description: Centralized SASE policy changes affect all connected users and sites. Audit logs enable compliance, change correlation, and accountability.
value: Security governance teams track all Check Point Harmony SASE administrative operations — policy installs, rule deletions, and permission changes — flagging high-impact modifications and out-of-hours activity that bypasses the change review process.
implementation: Forward audit logs via Log Exporter or Smart-1 Cloud. Alert on after-hours changes or policy publishes without change ticket. Correlate access anomalies with recent policy changes.
visualization: Table (recent changes), Timeline (admin activity), Bar chart (changes by admin).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Check Point Harmony SASE admin audit events in `index=checkpoint` with `sourcetype=checkpoint:harmony` or `sourcetype=checkpoint:audit`. Key fields: `administrator`, `operation` (Create/Modify/Delete/Login), `objecttype` (Rule/Network/User/Policy), `objectname`, `details`, `client` (SmartConsole/WebUI).
• Check Point audit tracks all administrative operations. Critical operations: policy install, rule deletion, blade disable, and admin permission changes.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify audit events:
```spl
index=checkpoint sourcetype IN ("checkpoint:harmony","checkpoint:audit") earliest=-30d
| where isnotnull(administrator) AND isnotnull(operation)
| stats count by operation, objecttype
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Admin audit trail:
```spl
index=checkpoint sourcetype IN ("checkpoint:harmony","checkpoint:audit") earliest=-7d
| where isnotnull(administrator)
| eval change_hour=strftime(_time, "%H")
| eval out_of_hours=if(change_hour<"08" OR change_hour>"18", "YES", "no")
| table _time, administrator, operation, objecttype, objectname, details, client, out_of_hours
| sort - _time
```

Understanding this SPL: Provides a complete admin audit trail with out-of-hours flagging. Policy installs are the highest-impact operation — they push changes live. Rule deletions may create security gaps. Admin permission changes may indicate privilege escalation.

Policy install tracking:
```spl
index=checkpoint sourcetype IN ("checkpoint:harmony","checkpoint:audit") operation="Install Policy" earliest=-30d
| stats count by administrator, objectname
| sort - count
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Make a test policy change. Verify audit event.
(b) In SmartConsole: Logs & Monitor > Audit.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — Admin operations per day. Row 2 — Policy installs. Row 3 — Out-of-hours changes.
Alert: Immediate on policy install or rule deletion outside change window.

Runbook:
1. **Policy install** — Verify change ticket. Confirm no disruption.
2. **Rule deletion** — Check dependencies. Restore if unintended.
3. **Admin permission change** — Verify authorization.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No audit events** — Log Exporter may not include audit events. Check export filter.
• **`operation` field generic** — Check Point uses specific audit event codes. Ensure proper parsing.
grandmaExplanation: We record every action administrators take in our Check Point security system — especially when they push new rules live or delete existing ones. Changes outside normal hours get special attention because a mistake at midnight can affect everyone by morning.
hardware: Check Point Harmony SASE management portal, Smart-1 Cloud
